meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a revenue-killing trap. To escape, adopt a modular SaaS starter kit that abstracts boilerplate. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you shift from passive consumption to active production, rapidly deploying a monetizable MVP while maintaining enterprise-grade code standards.
content.intro:
You have spent countless hours watching 'Build a SaaS' videos, yet your repository remains a graveyard of half-baked folders and abandoned ideas. You are stuck in the infinite loop of tutorial hell, a place where knowledge increases but your MRR stays at zero. It is time to stop being a student and start being a founder.

Real-world production isn't about perfectly typed components; it is about speed-to-market and solving high-value problems for paying customers. By integrating a battle-tested SaaS starter kit with AI-driven acceleration, you effectively bypass the trivial setup phase and focus entirely on the business logic that drives revenue.

steps.title: Architect the Foundation
steps.description: Define your SaaS architecture using Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate, ensuring authentication, billing, and database schemas are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS starter kit including Next.js, Supabase, Stripe billing, and Tailwind CSS. Include user role management and a protected dashboard route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize the project structure generated by Antigravity and set up the env variables'.

steps.title: Configure Identity and Access
steps.description: Secure your application immediately. A SaaS without robust authentication is a liability, not a business.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Supabase Auth with Google OAuth and email magic link providers. Implement a middleware check for protected routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the auth middleware and verify the user session handling in the dashboard folder'.

steps.title: Connect the Billing Engine
steps.description: Monetization is the primary objective. Connect Stripe to your starter kit to handle subscriptions before writing a single line of feature code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up Stripe webhooks for checkout sessions, subscription status, and customer portal redirection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate the Stripe webhook handler and create a subscription status check utility function.'

steps.title: Rapid Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on your core value proposition, ensuring that every new feature is built on top of your existing starter kit structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CRUD interface for a 'Projects' database table that links to the authenticated user ID.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Build the Projects model and the corresponding API endpoints for CRUD operations.'

steps.title: Deployment and Validation
steps.description: Ship to production immediately. Feedback from real users is the only metric that matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration and an automated testing script for the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Run the test suite and prepare the production build for deployment to Vercel.'

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use the starter kit's provider to avoid security vulnerabilities that kill startups.
Ship the billing page before the feature set; if users won't click 'Subscribe', your product is a hobby, not a business.
Use Claude Code to write tests for your Stripe webhooks; manual testing of payment flows is a recipe for disaster.
Keep your database schema lean; optimize for speed of change rather than future-proofing for scale you haven't reached yet.
Treat your starter kit as a product; document your own custom patterns so you can reuse them for your second and third SaaS.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop coding for 48 hours and focus exclusively on reaching out to your first 10 potential customers for feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint. The only variable remaining is your execution. Stop waiting for the perfect moment and start building the asset that will change your financial future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
